course_description:
This course provides access to and opportunity for student engagement in a global context. This project will provide participating students a global learning experience to learn about and experience in teaching and learning experience in a foreign country. The experience of being housed on a foreign university campus, interacting with colleagues, visiting a variety of educational institutions and sites, and engaging in discussions with professional networks will help our students to not only learn about the host country culture but to develop their intercultural competence, demonstrating the ability to work constructively with people of all backgrounds and orientations and understanding and respecting cultural issues. Visiting partner schools and tertiary institutions will allow students to view the variety of schooling practices and be engaged in discussions around school structures and policies for curriculum development, assessment, behaviour management, instructional pedagogies, and differentiated instruction. They will also visit heritage sites to develop a deeper understanding of the culture. The course is run through workshops and a field immersion abroad.

For this iteration, we will be learning about and experiencing the educational systems and cultures of Singapore.This course is connected to a New Colombo Plan Mobility Program. For those students who qualify for the Program and are selected, funding will be available to help defray some of the costs incurred for travel and participation.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
restrictions: Restricted to School of Education students receiving NCP funding. Required cumulative GPA of 5.0 and Course Coordinator approval.

timetable:
Additional timetable information
This is a 4 week immersion programme in Singapore. Students will be travelling to Singapore from 17 January 2026-13 February 2026.
course_aims: This course aims to provide School of Education students with a global learning experience: It will help them deepen their appreciation of multi-cultural perspectives, enable networking/collaboration with education students and providers in a different country, broaden their view of educational systems, immerse them in a different culture, and enrich their understanding of global educational issues.

learning_outcomes.description: Engage in professional dialogue with international counterparts.
Study current practices to improve educational outcomes and enhance their career development.
Participate in networking opportunities with international educators/students.
Describe the similarities and differences between the educational systems in the host country and Australia.
Appreciate the culture of the host country.

assessment_details.title: Reflective Journal
assessment_details.due_date: 20/02/2026 2:00 pm Reflective journal will be submitted a week after returning.
assessment_details.task_description: Assessment task one requires you to complete a reflective journal outlining your learning and engagement with a range of resources (presentations, readings, in-country experiences) across this course. Using the 'Five Rs' (Bain et al, 1999) to guide your reflections, you will undertake a process of thinking critically about your learning, your responses to the learning and how you will take that forward into your practice as a teacher in the future. You should be reflecting on each day of the planned field experiences course. Ultimately, you will submit five topical reflections. It is expected that each reflection should be about a page. We are looking primarily for how you will take what you learned and use it in your professional practice moving forward. The five topic areas are: Cultural visits (Cultural/Heritage Tour, Raffles Place/Singapore River) School Visits (SOTA, St. Joseph International, Raffles Girls Secondary) Informal Education Sites (Singapore Art Museum, National Gallery, Art Science Museum) Comparison of Education in Singapore and Australia : K-12 system AND Tertiary Initial Teacher Education Summary Reflection —looking back on the whole experience and including thoughts on impact of learning about the history, culture, educational systems, social aspects on your future career paths Five Rs (adapted from: Bain, Ballantyne, Packer & Mills, 1999) Reporting: what was the source of the learning? Responding: what were your feelings towards the issue? what was your initial response? Relating: how did the learning relate to your past and current personal and or professional experiences? why was it significant in the context of learning about Indigenous education? Reasoning: what theories are relevant in helping to understand and critically reflect on the issue? why do you think you had the initial response and what factors impacted on this? Reconstructing: why was this a significant learning experience for you as a teacher? how will you take this learning forward into practice? Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Please submit electronically via Blackboard and using Turnitin.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extension requests to be submitted via the apply for an extension hyperlink above, or through My requests on your my.UQ dashboard.
Late submission
There will be no grade penalty as this a pass/fail course

assessment_details.title: Group Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: 13/02/2026 Presentation times will be arranged during the overseas experience.
assessment_details.task_description: Working in a group of approximately 3 students, you will prepare a 15-to-20-minute presentation in which you will select an aspect of the field experience and describe how you could use that in your future classroom instruction. It could be something you learned about curriculum, pedagogical ideas, cultural inclusion, etc. Your group will need to: 1. Describe the field experience aspect chosen. 2. Explain how this could be used in your future instruction. 3. Relate the aspect to either your area of specialization (primary) or content area (secondary). 4. And be clear and engaging, with all group members equally participating in the presentation. You will have time in the afternoons and evenings to work on this. It will be presented to the group during our final debrief on our last day in Singapore. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
